goal: Make the 3 deck surfaces (profile/home/board picker) consistent, and stop the single-card result cropping off-ratio decks
done: Added getDecksForDisplay() in data.js (available-first, by unlock_day, NO active-reorder) used by profile My Decks, home strip, and the board 'Your deck' picker so all three match in order + unlock state (all via canUnlockDeck, gift-aware). Added an 'Active'/'ใช้อยู่' label to the picker (was border-only). Removed the active-jumps-to-front reorder on profile (glow + label only, per founder). Single daily/quick card now renders at the deck's NATURAL aspect ratio (img height:auto, dropped the hardcoded 1568/2720 container ratio + object-fit:cover) so off-ratio decks (Pawbit is 848x1264) aren't cropped at the frame — matches the share poster which sizes from naturalWidth/Height.
verified: NOT visually QA'd — browser busy; border fix needs a Dunkin check
notes: Unlock state is identical across surfaces (all canUnlockDeck); if Pawbit still shows locked on the board picker it's the stale iOS build (build 2 pending upload) or Cloudflare cache, not code. Reversed-art->flip, glow removal, and the question 1-card copy trim are separate queued follow-ups.
